expectations.text: Response explains running a separate itwillsync command per agent, both appearing on one phone dashboard
expectations.evidence: "Terminal 1 (Claude Code): npx --yes itwillsync -- claude" + "Terminal 2 (Aider): npx --yes itwillsync -- aider" + "Both sessions will appear as cards on your phone dashboard"

expectations.text: Response confirms the phone connection is fully bidirectional (can type and approve prompts, not just read-only)
expectations.evidence: "FULLY BIDIRECTIONAL CONTROL" section: "Full interactive terminal -- you can type commands, responses, and input just as if you were at the keyboard"

expectations.text: Response uses correct `--` separator syntax for both agent commands
expectations.evidence: Both commands use `--` separator: `npx --yes itwillsync -- claude` and `npx --yes itwillsync -- aider`

expectations.text: Response mentions the red-pulse attention indicator for identifying which session needs input
expectations.evidence: "ATTENTION INDICATOR: When an agent is waiting for your input, it typically sends a bell character. itwillsync detects this and makes the session card pulse red on the dashboard"
